title: Go upload-url remote-fetch safety parity plan

validation_order: _is_ssrf_target(hostname) rejects unresolvable, private, loopback, link-local, or reserved IPs
requests.get(url, headers=browser-like user-agent and referer, timeout=30, stream=True)
response body size must be <= MAX_CONTENT_LENGTH
magic.from_buffer first 2048 bytes must be image/jpeg, image/png, image/webp, or image/gif when magic validation succeeds
filename comes from secure URL basename or remote_<md5(url)[:8]> plus Content-Type extension
Pillow thumbnail writes _thumb.webp at max width 500 and quality 80 when available
success_response: data.filename is the returned local filename

thumbnail_only_contract.thumbnail_success: return _thumb.webp and do not retain original
thumbnail_only_contract.thumbnail_unavailable_or_failure: retain original image and return original URL

current_python_contract.known_python_runtime_detail_not_to_expan: Python currently reads response.content before size rejection even though stream=True; the Go candidate plan should enforce the size cap while streaming without changing Python in this gate.

go_candidate_plan.db_behavior: sqlite_query_only remains true because upload-url writes files only
go_candidate_plan.production_guard: production-like knowledge.db still requires PRISM_GO_ALLOW_PROD_DB=1; upload-url writes additionally require PRISM_GO_ALLOW_PROD_UPLOADS=1
go_candidate_plan.dependency_policy: reuse existing github.com/skrashevich/go-webp v0.1.0; add no new dependency without a separate decision
network_policy: reject unresolvable, private, loopback, link-local, multicast, unspecified, or reserved IPs
validate every redirect target before following it
set request timeout no longer than current Python 30 seconds
send deterministic User-Agent and Referer compatible with the Python route
read at most MAX_CONTENT_LENGTH + 1 bytes and fail before any file write when exceeded
response_parity_policy: match current 400 vs success behavior for empty URL, bad scheme, SSRF, download failure, non-image Content-Type, size overflow, and magic mismatch
standard success writes original plus _thumb.webp when thumbnail generation succeeds
thumbnail_only thumbnail failure keeps original as safe fallback
all validation failures leave data_dir/static/uploads unchanged

go_unit_tests: flag disabled route returns 405 and writes no files
DNS-resolved private host returns 400 with injectable resolver or test hook
redirect to private host is rejected
download request includes expected timeout and headers
non-image Content-Type returns 400 and writes no files
oversized response is capped while streaming and writes no files
magic mismatch returns 400 and writes no files
standard PNG/JPEG/WebP/GIF URL writes original and _thumb.webp
URL basename is sanitized and hash fallback is deterministic
python_vs_go_diff_fixtures: success response keys and thumbnail_only semantics
empty URL, bad scheme, SSRF, non-image, oversize, and magic mismatch error status class
packaging_smoke: copied-data smoke only; source knowledge.db and production uploads unchanged

not_authorized_by_23_8_thumb_6: Change SSRF behavior or remote request policy
Change upload delete or orphan cleanup ownership
Route frontend or Caddy upload traffic to Go
Write production DB or production uploads

allowed_next_step.title: Go upload-url local implementation candidate
allowed_next_step.summary: Implement only a disabled-by-default, copied-data POST /api/upload/url Go candidate behind its own flag, using the 23.8-thumb.6 safety/parity fixtures before any Pillow removal or live routing.
